reviews.review_msg: Silva was extremely dedicated to seeing each and everyone of her students succeed.  All you had to do was ask - her expectations were simple.  Follower her very detailed instructions for every assignment and you will do well.  She was absolutely opinionated, but this lent to her passion for the subject and for teaching students.

Very dissapointed in a professor who uses a 3rd-year lecture as her personal soap-box.  If you don't agree with her extreme-left ideologies and feminist rhetoric then don't bother. Her shock value goes beyond keeping students interested and crosses into the realm of distraction.  Good-luck focusing on the actual course content!

She is very intimidating and expects everyone to know what she's talking about just because we are "Communication" students. Her marking scheme is weird (how do you determine 0.25 of a mark?!). You might get screwed over if she marks your assignments in a bad mood, but at least you can always redo them for a remark.
great teacher, great class.  did not see a bias.  lot's of work.
She's pretty intimidating and very opinionated.  She's someone who needs control. You will do well in her course if you see her ahead of time about all your assignments otherwise, you're screwed.

Both phenomenal and crazy. 
 
 She loves to use really controversial topics for her examples. 
 Course requires hard work, but is the most practical course offered at SFU.

Difficult? Yes. Personal bias that she has no intention of hiding?Yes.  Talks about her outside business? Yup.  BUT, and this is important, the  only prof who will take time out to meet with you outside school hours so you can have a better grade/project.Take her with a grain of salt and L-E-A-R-N.

Worst class i've ever taken.  Silva obviously hated our class, and she did not make any point of trying to hide it.  She was not approachable, or friendly, or willing to help.  She was annoyed with the amount of emails she received, and she made you feel stupid if you didn't agree with her. Bah!

Since when has a prof's outside activities NOT gotten in the way of teaching?  The only reason everyone freaks out about Silva is because she talks about homosexuality... what's wrong with that?!  Oh no!  Cover your ears!

Yep, you either love her or you hate her. And I do agree that she talks about her own business too much. Since when is it a crime to be a feminist or to be gay? She is vocal about it, granted, but it only stands out because all of our other courses are taught with a "heterosexual agenda"
